---
title: "UNIONS-3500 Weak Lensing:<br>$B$-mode validation for cosmic shear"
occasion: Euclid Weak Lensing SWG telecon
description: "UNIONS-3500 B-mode validation for cosmic shear"
image: images/pure_eb_data_vector.png
author:
    name: Cail Daley
    affiliation: "CosmoStat, CEA Paris-Saclay  <br> <br> [on behalf of the UNIONS weak lensing team]{style=font-size:1.3em}"
date: April 27, 2026
date-format: long

bibliography: unions_bmodes.bib
csl: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/citation-style-language/styles/master/harvard-cite-them-right.csl
link-citations: false
suppress-bibliography: true

format:
    revealjs:
        width:  1920
        height: 1080
        theme: [default, custom.scss]
        slide-number: true
        fig-align: center
        menu: false
        include-after-body: assets/no_footer_on_titleslide.html
        footer: Cail Daley | Euclid WL SWG telecon | April 27, 2026
        history: false
        hash: true
        pdf-separate-fragments: true
        pdf-max-pages-per-slide: 1
        template-partials:
            - title-slide.html
        center-title-slide: false

html-math-method:
    method: mathjax
    url: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mathjax@3/es5/tex-svg-full.js
include-in-header: assets/mathjax.html
---

## Paper II of the UNIONS-3500 weak-lensing release

::: {style="margin-top:80px"}
- **Paper I** — Catalogues (Hervas-Peters et al.)
- **Paper II** — $B$-mode validation (this work)
- **Paper III** — Configuration-space cosmology (Goh et al.)
- **Paper IV** — Harmonic-space cosmology (Guerrini et al.)
- **Paper V** — Simulations and validation (Hervas-Peters et al.)
:::

::: {style="margin-top:80px"}
- UNIONS-3500: ${\sim}3500$ deg$^2$ of northern-sky cosmic shear ($n_\mathrm{eff} \approx 5.0$ arcmin$^{-2}$)
- The first deep, wide-area shape catalog in the north; CFHT MegaCam $r$-band shapes
- Paper II validates the fiducial scale cuts adopted for cosmological inference in Papers III and IV
:::


## At Stage-III sensitivities, $B$ modes are a systematics diagnostic

::: {style="margin-top:60px"}
- Scalar gravitational potential sources only $E$-mode power under the Born approximation [@stebbins96; @kamionkowski.etal97]
- Higher-order effects (lens-lens coupling, source clustering, intrinsic alignments) produce $B$ modes, but orders of magnitude below current sensitivity [@hilbert.etal09; @cooray.hu02]
- For galaxy shear at current sensitivity, **detected $B$-mode power indicates residual systematic effects rather than cosmological signal**
- Absence of $B$ modes does not rule out systematic contamination
:::

::: {style="margin-top:60px"}
Stage-III $B$-mode failures have traced:

- Repeating additive shear bias (CFHTLenS, KiDS-450, DES-SV; @asgari.etal19a)
- PSF fourth-moment leakage (HSC-Y3; @zhang.etal23)
- Astrometric systematics (KiDS-Legacy; @wright.etal25)
:::


## Three $E$/$B$-separable statistics

::: {style="margin-top:40px;font-size:0.95em"}
**Pure-mode correlation functions** $\xi_\pm^{\mathrm{E/B}}(\theta)$&nbsp;&nbsp;[— @schneider.etal22]{style="font-size:0.85em;opacity:0.8"}

- Integral transforms of $\xi_\pm(\theta)$ on a finite interval
- Decompose into $E$-mode, $B$-mode, and **ambiguous** components (boundary effects from the finite range)
:::

::: {style="margin-top:30px;font-size:0.95em"}
**COSEBIs** $E_n, B_n$&nbsp;&nbsp;[— @schneider.eifler.krause10; @asgari.schneider.simon12]{style="font-size:0.85em;opacity:0.8"}

- Discrete orthogonal modes; generalization of aperture-mass dispersion
- By construction, excludes ambiguous modes; captures cosmological information in the first ${\sim}6$ modes
:::

::: {style="margin-top:30px;font-size:0.95em"}
**Harmonic-space power spectra** $C_\ell^{EE}, C_\ell^{BB}$&nbsp;&nbsp;[— @wolz.etal25]{style="font-size:0.85em;opacity:0.8"}

- Catalog-based estimator (spin-2 SHT at source positions; no pixelization artifacts)
- NaMaster mode-coupling deconvolution; residual mask-induced $E{\to}B$ leakage absorbed into $C_\ell^{BB}$
:::

::: notes
Pure-mode and COSEBIs are defined on a finite angular interval and deproject ambiguous modes; the harmonic estimator does not. These three statistics represent B-mode power in complementary bases, and the mapping between them is not one-to-one.
:::


## Covariance

::: {style="margin-top:40px"}
- **Harmonic-space** ($C_\ell^{BB}$, $C_\ell^{EB}$): Gaussian covariance from NaMaster's iNKA framework
- **Configuration-space** (pure-mode, COSEBIs): semi-analytical \texttt{CosmoCov} covariance of $\xi_\pm$, propagated through the filter transforms
:::

::: {style="margin-top:30px"}
- COSEBIs: linear transformation, $\mathbf{C}_{B_n B_m} = \mathbf{T}^{\mathsf{T}} \mathbf{C}_{\xi} \mathbf{T}$
- Pure-mode: 2000 MC realizations of $\xi_\pm(\theta)$, propagated through the integral transforms, empirical covariance
:::

::: {style="margin-top:30px"}
- All $B$-mode null tests use Gaussian-only covariance (no connected trispectrum, no super-sample covariance)
- Validated on 350 GLASS mocks [@tessore.etal23]: all three statistics recover $B$ modes consistent with zero
- **Reported PTEs are therefore conservative**: a pass with Gaussian-only covariance would generally pass with a more complete estimate
:::


## Pure $E$/$B$ decomposition of the fiducial catalog

![](images/pure_eb_data_vector.png){width=82% fig-align="center"}

::: notes
E-modes (teal) trace the lensing signal; B-modes (crimson) diagnose systematics; ambiguous modes (purple) capture boundary effects. Shaded regions mark the adopted scale cuts.
:::


## COSEBI $B$-mode amplitudes

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:55%"}

![](images/cosebis_data_vector.png){width=100% fig-align="center"}

:::
::: {.column style="width:45%"}

::: {style="margin-top:120px;font-size:0.9em"}
- **Full range $1$–$250'$** (orange): $n{=}1$ shows $>4\sigma$ excess, oscillatory structure across twenty modes
- Pattern is consistent with repeating additive shear bias [@asgari.etal19a]
- **Fiducial cuts $12$–$83'$** (blue): first-mode excess below $1\sigma$; oscillations largely suppressed
- Modes $n \leq 6$ carry essentially all cosmological information
:::

:::
::::::::::::::


## Catalog-based harmonic-space power spectra

![](images/cl_data_vector.png){width=70% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="text-align:center;font-size:0.9em;margin-top:-10px"}
$C_\ell^{BB}/\sigma$ (top) and $C_\ell^{EB}/\sigma$ (bottom); gray shading marks $\ell = 300$–$1600$.
:::

::: notes
Wolz et al. 2025 catalog-based estimator — no pixelization, delta functions at source positions, NaMaster deconvolution. BB shows a low-level positive offset but passes within the fiducial range; EB scatters symmetrically around zero, so any B-mode contamination is not strongly correlated with the lensing signal.
:::


## Catalog versions: only the fiducial passes across all statistics

::: {style="font-size:0.9em;margin-top:20px"}
Four catalog variants, all from \texttt{ShapePipe} v1.4 (Paper I):
**Initial** — loose size cut $r_{h,\mathrm{gal}}/r_{h,\mathrm{psf}} > 0.5$;
**Fiducial** — tightened size cut $> 0.707$;
**Masked** — adds bright- and faint-star halo masks ($-13\%$ area);
**Relaxed-flags** — loosened \texttt{SExtractor} flags ($+23\%$ density).
:::

![](images/pte_table_appendix_paper3.png){width=85% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.8em;text-align:center;margin-top:4px"}
Bold values indicate PTE $< 0.05$ (null-test failure).
Refinements introduced after blinded validation showed early signs of systematic contamination (Paper I).
:::


## Scale-cut scan: adopted cuts sit in broad stable regions

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:38%"}

::: {style="margin-top:160px"}
- For each $(\theta_\mathrm{min}, \theta_\mathrm{max})$ pair, we compute $\chi^2$ against zero and assign a PTE
- Adopted cuts: $12$–$83$ arcmin (configuration), $\ell = 300$–$1600$ (harmonic)
- Cuts sit inside broad acceptance regions — moving them several bins in either direction does not change the outcome
- Conservative: we do not correct for look-elsewhere across scale-cut combinations
:::

:::
::: {.column style="width:62%"}

![](images/pte_cosebis_talk.png){width=100% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.75em;text-align:center;margin-top:-6px"}
COSEBIs PTE as a function of lower and upper scale cut.<br>Square: adopted cuts.
:::

:::
::::::::::::::


## Filter functions, not basis, set sensitivity to contamination

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:48%"}

::: {style="margin-top:40px;font-size:0.92em"}
- At full range, $\xi_\pm^{\mathrm{B}}$ and $C_\ell^{BB}$ pass; **COSEBIs fail** ($>4\sigma$ first mode, oscillations through all twenty modes)
- COSEBIs can be computed equivalently from $\xi_\pm$ or from $C_\ell$ bandpowers, via filter functions $W_n(\ell)$ (Fourier duals of $T_{\pm n}(\theta)$)
- **Both paths agree** — $C_\ell^{BB}$ passes while COSEBIs computed from the same $C_\ell^{BB}$ fail
- Sensitivity to contamination is set by the filter functions, not by the basis
- Empirical support for @asgari.etal19a and @jefferson.etal25: null tests in one representation cannot validate another
:::

:::
::: {.column style="width:52%"}

![](images/harmonic_config_cosebis_full.png){width=100% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.75em;text-align:center;margin-top:-6px"}
COSEBI $E_n$ (top) and $B_n/\sigma_n$ (bottom), computed from $\xi_\pm$ (filled circles)<br>and from $C_\ell$ bandpowers (open squares).
:::

:::
::::::::::::::

::: notes
The key methodological point of the paper. COSEBI filter functions W_n(ell) concentrate sensitivity on the contaminated scales; the unweighted bandpowers do not. Filter functions, not basis.
:::


## Repeating additive shear bias at CCD angular scales

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:55%"}

::: {style="margin-top:50px;font-size:0.95em"}
- @asgari.etal19a identified an oscillatory COSEBI pattern in CFHTLenS, KiDS-450, DES-SV — a spatially varying $c$-term from detector-level effects fixed in focal-plane coordinates
- **CFHTLenS used the same MegaCam camera**; its appearance in multiple Stage-III surveys points to a detector-level phenomenon rather than a pipeline-specific artifact
- MegaCam's 40 CCDs span $6$–$14$ arcmin individually; our lower cut of $12'$ excludes most pair separations at these scales
- UNIONS dithers by ${\sim}1/3$ field of view — larger than a single CCD — so each sky position averages the bias from several CCDs; may attenuate but is unlikely to fully eliminate the pattern
:::

:::
::: {.column style="width:45%"}

![](images/cosebis_data_vector.png){width=100% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.8em;text-align:center;margin-top:-6px"}
Orange: full range $B_n$ oscillations.<br>Blue: after adopted scale cuts.
:::

:::
::::::::::::::


## Implications for Stage-IV surveys

::: {style="margin-top:60px;font-size:1.0em"}
- Different detector geometry and dither strategies in *Euclid* and LSST may produce different $B$-mode signatures — but the underlying question (how each estimator responds to spatially coherent additive bias) is the same
- Pipeline-specific sources also matter: Paper V identifies improper propagation of exposure offsets within shape measurement, which can imprint a coherent additive shear pattern at CCD scales
- Because the same contamination projects differently into angular, modal, and multipole representations, **demanding passage in all three frameworks places tighter constraints than any single null test**
:::

::: {style="margin-top:40px"}
> *As surveys become systematics-limited, disagreements between $B$-mode estimators will carry as much information as their agreements; multi-framework validation will be needed to distinguish instrumental contamination, pipeline effects, and residual astrophysical signals.*
:::

::: {style="font-size:0.85em;text-align:right"}
— Paper II, Conclusions
:::


## Conclusions

<ul style="margin-top:40px">
<li style="margin-bottom:1.0em">UNIONS-3500 $B$ modes are **consistent with zero at the adopted cuts** across pure-mode correlation functions, COSEBIs, and harmonic-space power spectra</li>
<li style="margin-bottom:1.0em">At full range, all catalog versions show an **oscillatory COSEBI pattern consistent with repeating additive shear bias** at CCD scales — relevant for UNIONS because CFHTLenS also used MegaCam</li>
<li style="margin-bottom:1.0em">Of four catalog variants tested, **only the fiducial passes in every representation**</li>
<li style="margin-bottom:1.0em">COSEBIs from $\xi_\pm$ and from $C_\ell$ bandpowers agree — the disagreement is **not a matter of harmonic vs. real space**; filter functions set the sensitivity, not the basis</li>
<li>A $B$-mode failure in any one framework warrants scrutiny across all of them</li>
</ul>

::: {style="margin-top:40px;text-align:center"}
Paper II on arXiv soon — happy to discuss.<br>[cail.daley@cea.fr]{style="font-size:0.9em"}
:::


## Backup {background-color="black" style="color:white" visibility="uncounted"}


## Masking creates ambiguous modes that require $E/B$-separable statistics {visibility="uncounted"}

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:50%"}

Spin-2 shear fields can be decomposed into $E$-modes containing the lensing signal and $B$-modes, probing systematics at Stage-III noise levels.

<br>

In the presence of masking, some **ambiguous** modes cannot be cleanly attributed to $E$ or $B$.

$\implies$ need $E$/$B$-separable statistics

:::
::: {.column style="width:50%"}
![](images/emode_pattern.jpg){width=80%}
![](images/bmode_pattern.jpg){width=80%}
:::
::::::::::::::


## $B$-mode measurements across catalog versions {visibility="uncounted"}

![](images/pte_table_appendix_paper3.png){width=1700px fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.8em;text-align:center"}
$p$-values for four catalog versions across fiducial and full-range cuts.
Bold indicates PTE $< 0.05$. Only the fiducial catalog passes all statistics at the adopted cuts.
:::


## Configuration-space PTE maps by catalog version {visibility="uncounted"}

![](images/config_space_pte_fiducial.png){width=95% fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.8em;text-align:center"}
$\xi_+^B$, $\xi_-^B$, and COSEBI $B_n$ PTEs as a function of angular scale cuts, for the fiducial catalog.
Black squares mark the adopted cuts.
:::


## COSEBIs on GLASS mocks: bias $< 0.3\sigma$ per mode {visibility="uncounted"}

::: {style="margin-top:60px"}
- 350 lognormal simulated catalogs generated with \texttt{GLASS} [@tessore.etal23]
- Same survey footprint, galaxy positions, weights, and Planck 2018 cosmology as the data
- No PSF-related systematics — direct check that our estimators do not generate spurious $B$ modes
- Across the ensemble, all three statistics recover $B$ modes consistent with zero
- COSEBIs: mean $B_n$ biased by $<0.3\sigma$ per mode (100-realization subset)
- Scatter in simulated realizations comparable to fiducial error bars — uncertainty estimates of the right order
:::


## Covariance structure of the pure-mode data vector {visibility="uncounted"}

:::::::::::::: {.columns}
::: {.column style="width:40%"}

::: {style="margin-top:80px;font-size:0.95em"}
- Six blocks: $\xi_+^E$, $\xi_-^E$, $\xi_+^B$, $\xi_-^B$, $\xi_+^\mathrm{amb}$, $\xi_-^\mathrm{amb}$
- 20 angular bins per block
- Off-diagonal correlations arise from integral coupling in the pure-mode filters
- Captures cross-covariance between $\xi_+$/$\xi_-$, between $E$/$B$/ambiguous, and across angular bins
:::

:::
::: {.column style="width:60%"}

::: {style="margin-top:40px;text-align:center;font-size:0.9em"}
*(eb_covariance.png — to be exported from paper)*
:::

:::
::::::::::::::
